blocked.reason: Handoff: spec.md authored and discovery complete. Spec checkpoint NOT run — codex/gemini CLIs are unavailable in the remote container (FileNotFoundError). Resume locally: clear this block, then run checkpoint --slug user-roles --stage spec, reconcile findings, and continue into the plan stage. No open design decisions; discovery answers are recorded in state.json.

questions.question: Where do regular users create matches — a new user-facing flow, or by opening up the existing game-admin form?
questions.recommendation: New user-facing create-match action on the game pages, reusing the existing creation logic server-side.
questions.rationale: The admin form exposes admin-only knobs; a slim user flow keeps the admin surface unchanged.

questions.question: Where do regular users create matches — a new user-facing flow, or by opening up the existing game-admin form?
questions.recommendation: New user-facing create-match action on the game pages, reusing the existing creation logic server-side.
questions.rationale: The admin form exposes admin-only knobs; a slim user flow keeps the admin surface unchanged.

assumptions: users gains a role column ('admin' | 'user'); admin is seeded/synced at login from PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS, which remains the bootstrap source.
matches gains a nullable created_by_user_id; existing matches have no owner and are admin-managed only.
Owner cancel of a running match is allowed (cancel preserves data); owner delete is pre-start only.
Per-user active-match cap defaults to 3 and is configurable via an env var; admins are exempt.
Match/leaderboard viewing stays public; agent and connection ownership rules are unchanged.
discovery.summary: Two roles (admin/user) on the users table seeded from the existing platform-admin allowlist; match ownership via created_by_user_id; owner create/cancel anytime + delete pre-start; admin delete/cancel anything; per-user active-match cap.

answers.Where do regular users create matches \u: New user-facing create-match flow on the game pages, reusing existing server-side creation logic; admin form unchanged.
answers.When can a match owner delete their own: Owner can delete only matches that have not started; admins can delete any match in any state.

non_goals: No permissions tables, role hierarchies, or admin-management UI.
No change to viewing: matches and leaderboards stay publicly readable.
No change to the per-game admin (GAME_ADMIN_EMAILS) mechanism or the admin web forms.
No soft-delete/archive system; no tournaments or leagues.
acceptance_criteria: A signed-in regular user can create a match from a user-facing game-page flow; the match records them as creator.
A regular user can delete their own match only while it is pre-start, and can cancel their own running match; both are rejected for matches they do not own.
An admin can delete or cancel any match in any state.
A regular user cannot exceed the active-match cap; admins are exempt.
Users whose email is in PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS get role=admin on login; everyone else is role=user.

checklist.goal: Introduce admin vs regular user roles: signed-in regular users can create matches and delete/cancel their own; admins can delete or cancel any match.
checklist.audience: Signed-in players of Hoard Hurt Help (regular users) and platform operators (admins).
checklist.constraints: Async app (async routes + async DB). Schema change needs an Alembic migration that works on SQLite and Postgres. Admin role seeded from existing PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist. Per-user active-match cap to bound LLM spend. Game-admin email mechanism (GAME_ADMIN_EMAILS__<GAME>) stays as-is.
